'''Clan Ordo''' was a famed clan of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] who presided over the planet [[Ordo/Legends|Ordo]] in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]] for their Lord [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]], the ultimate official over the Mandalorians.

==History==
The most famed clan member was [[Canderous Ordo]], who fought during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], [[Mandalorian Wars]], [[Jedi Civil War]], and the [[Dark Wars]].

Canderous Ordo accompanied [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] during the Jedi Civil War and later, in accordance with orders from Revan and the interest of his people, reunited the Mandalorians under his banner as Mandalore. At some point between the years [[3954 BBY|3954]] and [[3951 BBY]], Canderous Ordo returned to the former Mandalorian [[Mandalorian Outpost (Dxun)|headquarters]] on the [[Onderon/Legends|Onderonian]] moon [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]] and began to reunite and rebuild Clan Ordo.<ref name="Chronicles of the Old Republic">{{LucasArts|url=games/swkotor_sithlords|text=''Chronicles of the Old Republic''|int=Chronicles of the Old Republic}}</ref><ref name="KotOR2" />

[[File:Jekiah_Ordo_and_Rass_Ordo.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jekiah Ordo and Rass Ordo]]
After the signing of the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], when [[Jicoln Cadera]] [[Crusader's schism|rebelled]] against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-favoring reign of [[Mandalore the Vindicated]],<ref name="Mandalore">{{HolonetOld|biographies|mandalore|Mandalore}}</ref> Clan Ordo was one of several clans that rallied to Cadera's side. Cadera and [[Clan Cadera|his clan's]] movement was eventually defeated; and Clan Ordo was then accepted back by [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="TOR:Kinship">{{TORcite|type=conversation|Torian Cadera|Kinship}}</ref> Clan Ordo would later on help train the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] bounty hunter [[Treek/Legends|Treek]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].

During the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]], the clan's members were split between the [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]], the [[True Mandalorians]], and the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the spy known as [[Bloodletter]] owned a pair of Mandalorian pistols that supposedly had belonged to the patriarch of Clan Ordo.<ref name="Thyferra: Bacta Basics">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031106thyferra1|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 1: The Thriving Season}}</ref>

By the time of the [[Sith–Imperial War]], [[Chernan Ordo]] had risen to hold the title of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']]. During the conflict, Ordo was hired by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] to defend the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Botajef/Legends|Botajef]] against an [[Battle of Botajef|impending attack]] by the [[Imperial Army/Legends|forces]] of the [[Fel Empire]], until the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet|Alliance's fleet]] arrived to repel the Imperials. However, the Alliance [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] never arrived, and Ordo [[Death/Legends|perished]] as his forces were overrun and defeated. Fellow Mandalorian [[Yaga Auchs]], who betrayed Ordo by [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaging]] their communications, usurped the title of ''Mand'alor'' and withdrew the Mandalorians from the conflict.

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', the flag found at Clan Ordo's headquarters on Dxun, is identical to that of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] (the symbol was most likely modeled after the Star Forge itself). The significance of its placement at that location is unknown. It is possible that Canderous adopted the emblem after the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]] as a memento of his part in the quest to destroy the [[Star Forge]]. It is also noted that several Clan Ordo members spoke with a New Zealand accent, following [[Temuera Morrison]].
